description:
Objective:
    You are a FinOps analyst who needs to set-up an automatic “daily cost report” that will help you keep cloud-spend under control.  
    Carry out the following steps from the Linux terminal (no root privileges required).

    1. Directory layout  
       • Create the directory /home/user/finops/scripts  
       • Create the directory /home/user/finops/logs  

    2. Reporting script  
       • Inside /home/user/finops/scripts create a file called daily_cost_report.sh  
       • It must begin with the standard bash she-bang  
         #!/bin/bash  
       • When the script runs it must append one single line to the log file /home/user/finops/logs/daily_cost_report.log.  
         The appended line must contain:  
           a) the literal text string  COST REPORT RUN AT  
           b) the current date-and-time in the ISO   YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS   format  
       • Example line (for illustration only):  
           COST REPORT RUN AT 2025-04-16 06:00:01  
       • Make the script executable for the current user.

    3. Cron job  
       • Add exactly ONE line to *your* user crontab that runs the script every weekday (Monday–Friday) at 06:00.  
       • The cron line must be:  
           0 6 * * 1-5 /home/user/finops/scripts/daily_cost_report.sh >> /home/user/finops/logs/daily_cost_report.log 2>&1  
       • Do NOT create additional cron entries.

    4. Human-readable summary (used for automated grading)  
       • After finishing, create /home/user/finops/setup_summary.txt containing exactly the three lines below (no extra spaces, no extra newlines):  
           ScriptPath:/home/user/finops/scripts/daily_cost_report.sh  
           Executable:yes  
           CronLine:0 6 * * 1-5 /home/user/finops/scripts/daily_cost_report.sh >> /home/user/finops/logs/daily_cost_report.log 2>&1  

    What the grader will check  
       • The directories exist as specified.  
       • /home/user/finops/scripts/daily_cost_report.sh is present, starts with the she-bang, contains the literal text “COST REPORT RUN AT”, and is executable.  
       • The user crontab contains exactly the single line given in step 3.  
       • /home/user/finops/setup_summary.txt exists and its content matches exactly the three lines shown above.  

    Do not include any explanatory comments or additional output in the summary file—the grader is strict about exact matches.
truth:
Expected artefacts and values after successful completion.

1. File system:
   a. Directory /home/user/finops/scripts must exist.
   b. Directory /home/user/finops/logs   must exist.

2. Script:
   Path: /home/user/finops/scripts/daily_cost_report.sh
   Permissions: at least 0755 (–rwxr-xr-x); executable bit for user must be set.
   Content requirements (substring checks):
     • First line exactly '#!/bin/bash'
     • Somewhere in the file the case-sensitive substring 'COST REPORT RUN AT' must appear.
     • The log target path must be '/home/user/finops/logs/daily_cost_report.log'

3. Crontab:
   The output of `crontab -l` for the current user must contain exactly one non-comment, non-blank line:
     0 6 * * 1-5 /home/user/finops/scripts/daily_cost_report.sh >> /home/user/finops/logs/daily_cost_report.log 2>&1
   No other active (non-comment) cron lines should be present.

4. Summary file:
   Path: /home/user/finops/setup_summary.txt
   Exact content (three lines, UNIX line endings):
     ScriptPath:/home/user/finops/scripts/daily_cost_report.sh
     Executable:yes
     CronLine:0 6 * * 1-5 /home/user/finops/scripts/daily_cost_report.sh >> /home/user/finops/logs/daily_cost_report.log 2>&1
